name: Azure ML Model as MCP Server
shortDescription: Deploy a trained ML model to Azure ML and expose it as an MCP server via APIM for Foundry agents.
detailedDescription: This lab demonstrates how to take a pre-trained sklearn forecasting model, deploy it to an Azure ML managed online endpoint, expose it as a REST API through Azure API Management, and wrap it as an MCP server for cloud-based agents in Azure AI Foundry. Includes built-in LLM logging for tracking token usage and tool calling, retry policies for resilience, and managed identity authentication.

name: Brain Tumour Classification Pipeline
shortDescription: An automated Azure ML Pipeline to automatically train and deploy a CNN model to classify brain scan images as having one of three types of tumour, or no tumour
detailedDescription: This lab demonstrates how pipelines can be set up on Azure ML. It first checks to see if the data, either training or testing, has changed at all (say if new data had been added). It then builds a data asset, and automatically trains the model (if the training data has changed). It then checks to see if the model is better, by testing the model on validation data. If so, the model is registered, and deployed to a batch endpoint to make predictions on the test dataset. The lab shows how large parts of an ML workflow can be automated using pipelines.

name: Conformal Energy Forecasting
shortDescription: Forecast hourly electricity demand with calibrated prediction intervals using Conformalized Quantile Regression (CQR) deployed via Azure ML batch endpoints.
detailedDescription: This lab demonstrates uncertainty-aware energy demand forecasting using Conformalized Quantile Regression (CQR). A multi-output MLP predicts lower, median, and upper quantiles of hourly electricity demand, then conformal prediction calibrates these into prediction intervals with formal finite-sample coverage guarantees. The full pipeline — synthetic data generation, feature engineering, model training with MLflow tracking, model registration with gating, and batch endpoint deployment — runs end-to-end on Azure Machine Learning.

name: C++ Model Training
shortDescription: Train a linear regression model in pure C++ with Azure ML pipeline orchestration.
detailedDescription: This lab proves that native C++ model training works on the Azure ML platform. It implements a linear regression model trained entirely in C++, orchestrated via an Azure ML pipeline. The pipeline handles data preprocessing, C++ compilation and execution, MLflow metric logging, and model registration — demonstrating subprocess orchestration for non-Python training workloads.

name: R Claims Severity Forecasting
shortDescription: Predict insurance claims severity using an R Gamma GLM deployed as an Azure ML batch endpoint.
detailedDescription: This lab demonstrates deploying a classical R statistical model on Azure Machine Learning. A Generalized Linear Model with Gamma family and log link — the standard actuarial approach for claims severity — is trained in R, registered as a custom model, and served via a batch endpoint. The pipeline generates synthetic motor insurance data, engineers features, fits the GLM with diagnostic metrics (AIC, BIC, deviance), gates deployment on test MAE, and produces predictions with 90% prediction intervals.
